blocks.text: DTC P0326: Knock Sensor output abnormal

The knock sensor uses a non-resonant type and is mounted on the cylinder block. When the cylinder block vibrates due to knocking, the weight applies pressure to the piezoelectric element and converts it into an electrical signal. The signal is then sent to the PGM-FI ECU to control the ignition timing. If the signal strength drops for a predetermined period of time or longer due to the knock sensor dropping out, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: *1: Cars not equipped with a turbo charger *2: Vehicles equipped with a turbo charger [ ]: HDS Parameters

blocks.text: If any of the following conditions persists for more than 5 seconds

items.text: At an engine speed of 1,000 rpm, the signal input value from the knock sensor is 30*. 2 For the following.
At an engine speed of 2,000 rpm, the signal input value from the knock sensor is 90*. 1 (130)* 2 For the following.
At 3,000 rpm [engine speed], the signal input value from the knock sensor is 190*. 1 (230)* 2 For the following.
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Engine speed [engine rpm] 1,800*. 1 （2,300）* 2 Runs for at least 5 seconds at rpm or higher.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
